description: Open-source framework for deploying AI agents with sandboxed execution, session persistence, streaming responses, and horizontal scaling
rules: Ash uses Bearer token authentication with API keys, not OAuth or JWT
Sessions persist across restarts using a three-layer storage architecture (live workspace, local snapshot, cloud snapshot via S3/GCS)
Streaming uses Server-Sent Events (SSE), not WebSockets
Each agent session runs in an isolated sandbox with restricted environment variables and resource limits
Ash scales horizontally with coordinators (control plane) and runners (data plane) that share state via Postgres/CockroachDB
The TypeScript SDK is @ash-ai/sdk, the Python SDK is ash-ai-sdk (auto-generated from OpenAPI spec)
Authorization is tenant-scoped via API keys. Role-based access control is implemented at the application layer using MCP sidecars and system prompt overrides
Use client.sendMessageStream() for streaming responses, not client.sendMessage()
